_comment: Dev/design seed data for LiteSkill VR. Loaded by `task dev:seed` into src-tauri/project.lsvr, and validated by a Rust test. Cross-references use local string keys (item `key`, ioi `key`, claim `key`); evidence targets are `self`, `claim:<key>`, or `finding:<ioi-key>`. author_type is a mix of human/agent to exercise the UI badges.

items.name: httpd
items.description: Main web server daemon. Speaks a custom HTTP-ish protocol on :8080.

notes.title: Entry points
notes.content: main() -> setup_sockets() -> accept_loop(). Each request is dispatched by type byte; login goes through parse_login().
notes.author: alice

iois.title: parse_login() stack overflow
iois.description: No bound check on the attacker-controlled length field before memcpy into a 256-byte stack buffer.
iois.author: demo-agent

iois.title: Password compared with strcmp
iois.description: Auth check uses strcmp on the secret — timing side-channel.
iois.author: alice

items.name: libfoo.so
items.description: Helper library: input validation, crypto helpers.

iois.title: validate_input() off-by-one
iois.description: Loop bound uses <= instead of <, writing one byte past the buffer.
iois.author: demo-agent

items.name: httpd.conf
items.description: Runtime config. Contains a debug flag and a default admin token.

iois.title: Hardcoded admin token
iois.description: admin_token=CHANGEME shipped by default; accepted by the auth path.
iois.author: alice

items.name: init.sh
items.description: Boot script that launches httpd as root.

connections.description: init script starts httpd as a daemon
connections.author: alice

explanations.title: Authentication flow
explanations.summary: Client sends HELLO; server returns a nonce; client returns a signed nonce; on valid signature the session moves to AUTHENTICATED and privileged commands are accepted.
explanations.author: demo-agent

claims.text: Signature verification uses RSA-2048 against a baked-in public key.
LoginRequest.length describes payload bytes, not total packet size.

open_questions.question: Is LoginRequest.length bounded before the payload copy in parse_login()?
Does a failed signature verification always return before the state update?

states.name: UNAUTHENTICATED
states.description: No session key; only HELLO is accepted.

states.name: CHALLENGE_SENT
states.description: Server has issued a nonce and is awaiting a signed response.

evidence.excerpt: attacker-controlled length flows into memcpy size

explanations.title: Component architecture
explanations.summary: init.sh spawns httpd (root); httpd links libfoo.so, reads httpd.conf, and listens on :8080. Trust boundary at the network socket.
explanations.author: alice

claims.text: The only remote attack surface is the :8080 TCP listener in accept_loop().

explanations.title: Wire protocol overview
explanations.summary: Length-prefixed binary protocol: 4-byte magic, 1-byte type, 2-byte length, payload. Types: HELLO(1), LOGIN(3), CMD(4).

claims.text: Messages are framed by the 2-byte length field; there is no overall packet checksum.

explanations.title: LoginRequest packet format
explanations.summary: magic(u32)=0x4c534b4c, type(u8)=3, length(u16, payload bytes), payload(bytes). length and payload are attacker-controlled.

claims.text: length is attacker-controlled and is used directly as the memcpy size in parse_login().

open_questions.question: Is there an upper bound enforced on length anywhere?

explanations.title: parse_login control flow
explanations.summary: read header -> validate magic -> switch(type) -> copy payload into stack buffer -> dispatch. The copy precedes any length check.

explanations.title: Attacker length to memcpy (taint)
explanations.summary: Source: network packet length field. Path: parse_login reads length -> passes as size to memcpy into a 256-byte stack buffer. Sink: stack overflow. No validation on the path.

claims.text: No bounds check exists between the length read and the memcpy sink.

explanations.title: Session context memory layout
explanations.summary: session_ctx struct (0x40 bytes): nonce[16] @0x00, state(u32) @0x10, key[32] @0x14. The 256-byte login buffer sits just below the saved return address.

explanations.title: Session object lifecycle
explanations.summary: alloc on accept() -> initialized in handle_hello -> freed on disconnect. No use-after-free observed yet; double-free on error path is unverified.

open_questions.question: Can the error path free the session twice?

explanations.title: libfoo.so exported API
explanations.summary: Exports validate_input(buf,len), decrypt(buf,len,key), and hash(buf,len). validate_input has an off-by-one; decrypt uses a fixed IV.

explanations.title: Pre-auth threat model
explanations.summary: Unauthenticated remote attacker on the LAN can reach :8080. Highest-value path: LoginRequest parsing (pre-auth memory corruption). Secondary: default admin token.

claims.text: The most severe bug is reachable before authentication.

explanations.title: Misc reversing notes
explanations.summary: Scratch space for findings that don't fit a structured type yet: anti-debug check at start(), strings table at 0x09000, suspected watchdog thread.
